findings.title: Clone-from-share copies full day-plan text, silently bypassing the owner's "Show day-by-day plans" privacy toggle
findings.rootCause: fetch_share_payload (public.py:591-621) gates plan SELECT/render on share_show_plans, but the clone path does NOT. clone_trip_from_share_token (trips.py:1519) calls the shared _clone_trip_record, which unconditionally SELECTs morning/afternoon/evening/tip (trips.py:1384-1389) and INSERTs them into the new trip's days (trips.py:1407-1425). The function takes no plan-gating parameter and never reads share_show_plans. The Share modal markets the toggle as a privacy control (en.ts togglePlansBody: 'Morning / afternoon / evening notes per day. Photos and documents stay private.'), so the bypass directly violates the stated contract.

findings.title: Cloned to-do places keep dangling dayId/timeOfDay pointing at the source trip's days — invisible on every per-day map view (sibling of the just-fixed template bug)
findings.rootCause: _clone_trip_record copies marked_places_json VERBATIM (trips.py:1307-1309) while generating brand-new day ids for the cloned days (trips.py:1393, 1414). MarkedPlace.dayId (and AI timeOfDay) therefore reference the SOURCE trip's day ids, which do not exist in the clone. todoMarkers.ts:183 hides any place whose dayId != selectedDayId when on a numbered day, and the day chip lookup days.find(d => d.id === place.dayId) (todoMarkers.ts:143) returns nothing. This is the exact bug just fixed for Trip Templates, where _clean_marked_places (templates.py:85-109) strips dayId/timeOfDay precisely because 'a trip created from a template has brand-new day ids ... those references are dangling'. The clone path was never given the same cleaning.

findings.title: Clone references the source owner's cover file; the clone's cover 404s when the original owner deletes their trip or changes the cover
findings.rootCause: _clone_trip_attempt copies src['cover_url'] verbatim (trips.py:1320), so the clone's cover_url points at the ORIGINAL owner's uploaded file under /static/uploads/<owner_id>/<file>. When the owner deletes their trip, delete_trip collects that exact path (collect_trip_upload_paths includes cover_url, helpers.py:78-80) and delete_upload_files physically os.remove()s it (helpers.py:121-163, called at trips.py:455) with NO check for other trips/clones still referencing the file. The same break occurs on cover removal/replacement via upsert_trip. Files are also served read-any to signed-in users, so this is purely a dangling-reference/availability bug, but the clone permanently depends on an asset it doesn't own.

findings.title: Revoked / expired / archived share page still renders the "I want this trip" clone CTA that then fails with a generic error
findings.rootCause: The .share-cta block including the clone link (share.html:379-393) is OUTSIDE the {% if days %}/{% if cost %} guards, and the 404 render path in main.py:1085-1101 still passes canonical_url, so the CTA href '/?cloneFromShare=<token>' is emitted on the unavailable page too. clone_trip_from_share_token returns 404 (revoked/deleted) or 410 (archived) (trips.py:1548-1553), and attemptPendingClone surfaces only the generic t('errors.cloneFailedFromCollections') (clone-intent.ts:48-51) with no distinct 'this trip is no longer available' message.

findings.title: Public share page is hardcoded English despite full en/es/fr/pt app support
findings.rootCause: share_page (main.py:1072) renders share.html with no locale resolution; <html lang="en"> is hardcoded (share.html:2) and all visible copy is literal English in the template. There is no Accept-Language handling and no server-side string table (unlike the PDF route, which got server-side i18n in PDF-5). The /t/<code> template-preview page shares the same English-only posture, so this is at least consistent, but it remains a real gap for a public, shareable surface.

findings.title: Bulk /api/sync lets a non-owner planner write trips.is_archived — the share/clone gate's source of truth — diverging from the owner-only mirror the dedicated archive route enforces
findings.rootCause: The dedicated archive_trip/unarchive_trip routes mirror trips.is_archived ONLY when is_trip_owner is true (trips.py:491-497, 581-587). But the bulk /api/sync active-trips ON CONFLICT writes is_archived=excluded.is_archived unconditionally (data.py:340) from the caller's payload value (data.py:380 `1 if t.get('is_archived') else 0`), gated only by editable_trip_ids — which includes non-owner planners. The first-party frontend now sends an empty /api/sync body so this is dormant for current clients, but the endpoint still accepts a crafted {"trips":[{"id":...,"is_archived":0|1}]} payload from any authenticated editor, and the archive source-of-truth (trips.is_archived) used by the share gate is decoupled from the per-member trip_members.is_archived the rest of the app uses.
